title: Container App Model
description: Schema for JSON-driven Azure Container App definitions consumed by environments/<env>/main.tf. NOTE: Key-Vault-backed secrets (e.g. API keys) are NOT configurable through this model — secret material can't live in a committed JSON file, so any secret env vars a container app needs are wired by hand in environments/dev/main.tf, matched by this model's logical key. env here only covers plain, non-secret values.

container_apps.description: Map keyed by a stable logical id. The key becomes the for_each key in Terraform.

resource_group_key.description: Logical id of the resource group (key under resource_groups in resource-group.json) to deploy into.

container_app_environment_key.description: Logical id of the environment (key under container_app_environments in container-app-environment.json) this app runs in.

container_registry_key.description: Logical id of the registry (key under container_registries in container-registry.json) to pull the image from.

container_name.description: Name of the container in the app's template.

image_name.description: Image repository name within the registry, e.g. "chatbot-backend" for <registry-login-server>/chatbot-backend:<image_tag>.

image_tag.description: Update via CI after pushing a new image; Terraform does not build or push images itself.

cpu.description: vCPU allocation. 0.25 is the smallest allowed Consumption-plan size.

memory.description: Must pair with cpu per Azure's allowed combinations.
